title: Primordial black holes as dark matter
statement: Black holes could have formed in the very early universe from the collapse of large density fluctuations, before stars existed (Hawking 1971; Carr-Hawking 1974). Such primordial black holes (PBHs) are a candidate for some or all of the dark matter, requiring no new particle. Observational limits (Hawking-evaporation constraints, microlensing, dynamical and accretion bounds, gravitational waves) exclude PBHs as the dominant dark matter across most mass ranges, but an 'asteroid-mass' window (~10^17-10^23 g) remains largely open, and PBHs could still be a sub-component or seed SMBHs. Whether PBHs exist at all, and their cosmological role, is unresolved.

originators.name: Stephen Hawking
originators.note: 1971 proposal that gravitationally-collapsed objects could form in the early universe.

originators.name: Bernard Carr
originators.note: with Hawking, 1974, developed PBH formation from density perturbations.

ranking.rationale: Cosmology/observation-driven and old (1971), but progressing fast with microlensing, GW, and CMB constraints; needs no new fundamental physics, so it ranks below the quantum-gravity problems despite its age.

key_ideas: PBHs form when a sufficiently large primordial overdensity re-enters the horizon and collapses; the mass is set by the horizon mass at that epoch.
Mass spectrum is broad in principle (from sub-gram to supermassive), so constraints are window-by-window.
Hawking evaporation: PBHs below ~5e14 g would have evaporated by now; their final bursts are searched for (gamma rays).
Microlensing (MACHO/EROS/OGLE/Subaru-HSC), dynamical heating of star clusters, CMB accretion, and the LIGO/Virgo merger population all set bounds.
The asteroid-mass window (~10^17-10^23 g) is hard to constrain (lensing wave-optics + finite-source effects) and remains viable for 100% dark matter.
PBHs could also be sub-dominant yet seed supermassive black holes (#12) or contribute to GW mergers.

approaches.name: Microlensing surveys
approaches.summary: Search for brightening of background stars by PBH lenses to constrain the mass function.

approaches.name: Evaporation / gamma-ray constraints
approaches.summary: Bound low-mass PBHs via Hawking emission and final-burst signatures.

approaches.name: GW and accretion constraints
approaches.summary: Use the LIGO/Virgo merger population and CMB accretion bounds to limit PBH abundance.

papers.title: Gravitationally collapsed objects of very low mass
papers.note: Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society, 152, 75 (1971). The original proposal that black holes could form from inhomogeneities in the early universe — the conceptual origin of primordial black holes. Canonical landmark included from certain knowledge; routinely cited in the modern reviews surfaced by search.

papers.title: The hypothesis of cores retarded during expansion and the hot cosmological model
papers.note: Soviet Astronomy, 10, 602 (1967). First suggestion that black holes could form from strong density perturbations in the expanding early universe, predating and motivating Hawking's PBH proposal. Canonical landmark included from certain knowledge.

papers.title: Black holes in the early Universe
papers.note: Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society, 168, 399 (1974). Established the conditions for PBH formation from collapsing overdensities during radiation domination and the horizon-mass relation. Canonical landmark.

papers.title: The primordial black hole mass spectrum
papers.note: The Astrophysical Journal, 201, 1 (1975). Derived the PBH mass function from the collapse of primordial density fluctuations and the threshold for collapse; the founding quantitative treatment of PBH abundance. Canonical landmark.

papers.title: Constraints on primordial black holes
papers.note: Reports on Progress in Physics, 1060 citations. The definitive modern compilation of constraints on the PBH dark-matter fraction across the full mass range.

papers.title: Primordial black holes as dark matter candidates
papers.note: SciPost Physics Lecture Notes, 159 citations. Review of formation, evaporation, and the surviving mass windows where PBHs could be all of dark matter.

papers.title: Primordial black holes as a dark matter candidate
papers.note: Journal of Physics G, 662 citations. Concise, widely cited status review of PBH dark matter circa 2020 covering formation and current/future constraints.

papers.title: A Brief Review on Primordial Black Holes as Dark Matter
papers.note: Frontiers in Astronomy and Space Sciences, 177 citations. Accessible review of formation, abundance, evaporation, accretion, and observational probes.

papers.title: Threshold for primordial black holes: Dependence on the shape of the cosmological perturbations
papers.note: Physical Review D, 231 citations. Numerical determination of how the collapse threshold depends on the perturbation profile — central to abundance calculations.

papers.title: Did LIGO Detect Dark Matter?
papers.note: Physical Review Letters, 917 citations. Sparked the modern PBH-dark-matter revival by proposing the LIGO BH binaries could be ~30 solar-mass PBHs.

papers.title: Microlensing constraints on primordial black holes with Subaru/HSC Andromeda observations
papers.note: Nature Astronomy, 541 citations. Dense-cadence HSC M31 survey setting stringent microlensing limits on light PBHs, a key probe of the asteroid-mass window edge.

papers.title: Microlensing and dynamical constraints on primordial black hole dark matter with an extended mass function
papers.note: Physical Review D, 139 citations. Shows extended mass functions do not evade combined microlensing and dynamical constraints in the stellar-mass range.

papers.title: Updated constraints on asteroid-mass primordial black holes as dark matter
papers.note: Physical Review D, 108 citations. Finite-source effects weaken HSC microlensing limits, broadening the open asteroid-mass window.

papers.title: Revisiting constraints on asteroid-mass primordial black holes as dark matter candidates
papers.note: JCAP, 235 citations. Reanalyzes femtolensing, white-dwarf, neutron-star, and microlensing bounds and argues the asteroid-mass window (~10^-16 to 10^-12 solar masses) remains open for 100% PBH dark matter.

papers.title: How open is the asteroid-mass primordial black hole window?
papers.note: SciPost Physics, 20 citations. Recomputes current and future evaporation and microlensing bounds with improved extended-mass-function fits; the window narrows but stays open unless the MF is wide.

papers.title: Primordial black hole constraints with Hawking radiation — A review
papers.note: Progress in Particle and Nuclear Physics, 67 citations. Up-to-date review of evaporation-based constraints on light/asteroid-mass PBHs.

papers.title: Breakdown of Hawking Evaporation opens new Mass Window for Primordial Black Holes as Dark Matter Candidate
papers.note: MNRAS, 79 citations. Argues semiclassical Hawking evaporation breaks down after half-decay, potentially reopening a sub-10^10 g window for PBH dark matter.

papers.title: CMB bounds on disk-accreting massive primordial black holes
papers.note: Physical Review D, 219 citations. CMB-anisotropy constraints from accretion exclude monochromatic PBHs above ~2 solar masses as dominant dark matter.

papers.title: Cosmic microwave background bounds on primordial black holes including dark matter halo accretion
papers.note: Physical Review Research, 172 citations. Refines CMB accretion bounds with DM-halo accretion around PBHs using Planck data.

papers.title: Gravitational waves from primordial black hole mergers
papers.note: JCAP, 305 citations. Computes the PBH binary merger rate and derives stochastic-GW-background constraints on f_PBH in the 0.5–30 solar-mass range.

papers.title: Primordial black holes from single field models of inflation
papers.note: Physics of the Dark Universe, 526 citations. Shows a near-inflection point in single-field inflation can produce a power-spectrum peak yielding PBH dark matter.

papers.title: Steepest growth of the power spectrum and primordial black holes
papers.note: JCAP, 335 citations. Derives the analytic maximum growth rate (n_s - 1 = 4) of the inflationary power spectrum, bounding PBH formation scenarios.

papers.title: Primordial black holes as generators of cosmic structures
papers.note: MNRAS, 245 citations. Explores PBHs as seeds (and Poisson generators) of cosmic structure and supermassive black holes even at low abundance.

papers.title: Recent Gravitational Wave Observation by Pulsar Timing Arrays and Primordial Black Holes: The Importance of Non-Gaussianities
papers.note: Physical Review Letters, 146 citations. Shows PBH overproduction disfavors Gaussian scalar-induced-GW explanations of the NANOGrav 15-yr signal, linking PTA data to PBH formation.

papers.title: Constraints on primordial black holes from LIGO-Virgo-KAGRA O3 events
papers.note: Physical Review D, 38 citations. Hierarchical-Bayesian limits from LVK O3 constrain f_PBH below ~10^-3 over 1–200 solar masses across many mass-function models.

physicists.name: Bernard J. Carr
physicists.contribution: Co-founder of PBH theory (Carr & Hawking 1974, Carr 1975); derived the PBH mass spectrum and leads the modern constraint compilations.

physicists.name: Stephen W. Hawking
physicists.contribution: Proposed that black holes could form from early-universe inhomogeneities (1971) and that small ones evaporate via Hawking radiation, defining the light-PBH constraints.

physicists.name: Yakov B. Zel'dovich
physicists.contribution: With Novikov, first suggested black holes could form from strong density perturbations in the expanding early universe (1967).

physicists.name: Igor D. Novikov
physicists.affiliation: Astro Space Center, Lebedev Physical Institute / Niels Bohr Institute
physicists.contribution: Co-author of the original Zel'dovich-Novikov proposal for early-universe black-hole formation.

physicists.name: Anne M. Green
physicists.contribution: Leading authority on PBH dark-matter constraints, extended mass functions, and the status of the asteroid-mass window.

physicists.name: Juan García-Bellido
physicists.contribution: Inflationary PBH formation (inflection-point single-field models) and reanalysis of MACHO/EROS microlensing for PBH dark matter.

physicists.name: Marc Kamionkowski
physicists.contribution: Co-author of 'Did LIGO Detect Dark Matter?', reviving the stellar-mass PBH-dark-matter hypothesis after GW150914.

physicists.name: Simeon Bird
physicists.contribution: Lead author proposing LIGO's ~30 solar-mass binaries as PBH dark matter and developing the PBH merger-rate framework.

physicists.name: Ilia Musco
physicists.affiliation: Sapienza University of Rome / Observatoire de Paris
physicists.contribution: Numerical relativity of PBH collapse; established profile-dependent formation thresholds and critical collapse relations.

physicists.name: Kazunori Kohri
physicists.affiliation: KEK, High Energy Accelerator Research Organization, Japan
physicists.contribution: Co-author of the canonical 2020 constraints compilation and of CMB-accretion and BBN/evaporation bounds on PBH abundance.

simulator_angle: Asteroid-mass PBHs are far too small to image, but the simulator's Hawking-evaporation module (thermo) directly gives the evaporation lifetime that sets the low-mass PBH constraint boundary (~5e14 g evaporating now).

metadata.coverage_note: Assembled 24 verified papers and 10 verified physicists. Method: ran 11 Consensus paper-search calls (mcp__plugin_bio-research_consensus__search) across the problem's sub-themes — PBH-as-DM reviews, formation/inflation/power-spectrum, threshold and mass-function/critical-collapse, microlensing (HSC, MACHO/EROS), asteroid-mass window, Hawking-evaporation bounds, CMB accretion, LIGO/PTA gravitational-wave constraints, and SMBH-seed/structure-formation. 20 papers come directly from search results and carry their exact Consensus URLs in identifier.url. The remaining 4 are canonical originator landmarks included from certain knowledge with bibliographic notes (and DOIs where applicable): Zel'dovich & Novikov 1967, Hawking 1971, Carr & Hawking 1974, and Carr 1975 — the founding PBH papers, all routinely cited in the modern reviews surfaced by search. No titles, authors, years, DOIs, or URLs were invented; full author lists for a few large-collaboration search papers are abbreviated with 'et al.' Tiers reflect the open-problem status: review/constraint papers are tagged observational_constraint, resolution-level "PBHs are the dark matter" claims open_conjecture, evaporation-window reopenings speculative_extension, and exact-GR/analytic results analytic_classical. This is an honest count, not padded to 25.
